metadata.title: Mobile-platform accessibility review sub-rubric
metadata.description: Mobile-specific accessibility criteria layered atop the universal base a11y rubric. Covers VoiceOver/TalkBack labelling, Dynamic Type / scalable text, touch-target sizing (>= 44pt iOS / >= 48dp Android), reduced motion, haptic-feedback alternatives, high-contrast mode, and switch-control compatibility.

severity_rules.pattern: Custom interactive control (button, switch, segmented control) lacks accessibilityLabel (iOS) / contentDescription (Android); icon-only controls are announced as 'button, button' with no semantic intent.
severity_rules.description: VoiceOver and TalkBack users navigate by spoken labels; unlabelled controls are unusable. WCAG 2.1 SC 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) is part of MFi accessibility certification.
severity_rules.remediation: Set accessibilityLabel / contentDescription on every interactive element; describe the action, not the icon ('Play episode' not 'Play button').

severity_rules.pattern: Decorative imagery is exposed to assistive tech; or grouped controls (composite cell, toolbar) are not exposed as a single navigable element.
severity_rules.description: Decorative content adds noise to assistive-tech users; ungrouped composites force them to swipe through every sub-element to reach the action.
severity_rules.remediation: Set isAccessibilityElement = false on decorative images; mark composites with shouldGroupAccessibilityChildren / mergeDescendants and a unified accessibilityLabel.

severity_rules.pattern: App uses fixed point sizes (UIFont.systemFont(ofSize: 14), `12sp`) instead of preferredFont(forTextStyle:) / scaledFont; text does not respond to user's Dynamic Type / Font-Size system setting.
severity_rules.description: Roughly 25% of mobile users use a non-default text-size setting; ignoring it forces low-vision users into a smaller-than-needed text size that they cannot read.
severity_rules.remediation: Use preferredFont(forTextStyle:) / UIFontMetrics.scaledFont (iOS) or `sp` units with auto-sizing on Android; test with the largest accessibility text size enabled.

severity_rules.pattern: Layout breaks (text truncates, controls overlap) when Dynamic Type is set above the default; layout uses fixed-height containers instead of intrinsic content sizing.
severity_rules.description: Truncated labels are unusable when the user cannot read them. Fixed-height layouts that ignore content height fail accessibility testing.
severity_rules.remediation: Use Auto Layout with content-hugging / compression-resistance priorities; allow vertical growth; test with the AX1..AX5 sizes enabled.

severity_rules.pattern: Interactive control measures less than 44pt x 44pt on iOS or 48dp x 48dp on Android; users with motor impairments or large fingers cannot reliably hit it.
severity_rules.description: Apple HIG and Android Material Design both mandate a minimum tap area of 44pt / 48dp respectively. WCAG 2.5.5 (Target Size, AAA) reinforces the threshold for accessibility.
severity_rules.remediation: Set the visual control to its design size and expand the hit-test area via hitInsets / increased ImageButton padding to reach 44pt / 48dp.

severity_rules.pattern: Touch targets are spaced too closely — less than 8pt / 8dp between adjacent interactive controls — causing accidental taps for users with reduced dexterity.
severity_rules.description: Spacing failures cause accidental selections that are especially harmful in destructive actions (delete, send, pay).
severity_rules.remediation: Maintain 8pt / 8dp minimum spacing between interactive controls; group related actions in segmented controls instead of dense grids.

severity_rules.pattern: App ships parallax / heavy motion animations without honoring UIAccessibility.isReduceMotionEnabled (iOS) / Settings.Global.TRANSITION_ANIMATION_SCALE (Android); users with vestibular disorders see motion they cannot disable.
severity_rules.description: Vestibular-disorder users get motion sickness from parallax; ignoring the system setting is a documented WCAG 2.1 SC 2.3.3 failure.
severity_rules.remediation: Honour the system setting; replace motion transitions with fade or instant transitions when reduce-motion is on.

severity_rules.pattern: App uses haptic feedback (Taptic Engine, HapticFeedbackConstants) as the sole signal for state change (success, error, selection); deaf-blind or hearing-impaired users on devices without haptics get no feedback.
severity_rules.description: Haptics are an enhancement, not a substitute. Sole-channel feedback fails users on devices without haptic hardware or with haptics disabled.
severity_rules.remediation: Pair haptics with a visual cue (animation, colour change) and an accessibility announcement (UIAccessibility.post(notification: .announcement, …)).

severity_rules.pattern: Colour pairs fail WCAG 4.5:1 contrast in the user's selected colour scheme (Increase Contrast / High Contrast mode); pure-grey separators or low-contrast hint text disappear under high-contrast.
severity_rules.description: Both platforms expose a high-contrast preference; not honouring it breaks low-vision use of the app even when standard contrast is fine.
severity_rules.remediation: Subscribe to UIAccessibility.darkerSystemColorsStatusDidChangeNotification / AccessibilityManager.isHighTextContrastEnabled and swap palettes; verify with platform contrast checkers.
